sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 NUMERIC, value2 NUMERIC, category VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, category) VALUES (1, 5, 10, 'A'), (2, 10, NULL, 'B'), (3, NULL, 20, 'C'), (4, 12.5, 25.5, 'D'), (5, 0, 0, 'E');
SELECT COVAR_POP(value1, value2) AS covariance FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL OR value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT category, COVAR_POP(value1, value2) AS covariance FROM test_data GROUP BY category HAVING COVAR_POP(value1, value2) IS NOT NULL OR category = 'A';
SELECT COVAR_POP((CASE WHEN value1 IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE value1 END), (CASE WHEN value2 IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE value2 END)) AS covariance FROM test_data;
SELECT COVAR_POP(value1, value2) AS covariance FROM (SELECT value1, value2 FROM test_data WHERE value1 > 5 OR value2 < 15) AS filtered_data WHERE filtered_data.value1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COVAR_POP(value1, value2) AS covariance FROM (SELECT id, value1, value2 FROM test_data WHERE id < 3 OR id > 3) AS subquery WHERE value1 OR value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COVAR_POP(value1, value2) AS covariance FROM test_data GROUP BY category HAVING COVAR_POP(value1, value2) > (SELECT AVG(COVAR_POP(value1, value2)) FROM test_data);
